data.body:
DESIGN BRAINSTORM — open→in_progress "plan-review" verification gate (mirror of the completion-verifier close gate). Status: design largely converged; tuning + write-up of design-of-record pending DSO data mining.

GOAL: a verification workflow that runs when a ticket is claimed (open→in_progress), reviewing the ticket's implementation plan BEFORE an agent executes it — the inverse of the completion-verifier (which runs at close). It is a rebar FEATURE used by DIVERSE CLIENT PROJECTS (many languages/frameworks/structures); design judged by cross-language generalization, NOT rebar's own codebase. (Correction logged: rebar is Python with legacy bash being strangled; an earlier "rebar is heavily bash" claim was a conflation with DSO and was deleted.)

CONVERGED DESIGN (two-layer):
- Trigger/enforcement: fires at open→in_progress. `claim` = FAST check for a valid signed plan-review attestation; the heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND. It is a REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the CLIENT revises the plan and RE-RUNS the review to earn a fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier: returns FAIL+findings, blocks close, client fixes+recloses). rebar owns the review/verdict/findings/signature/invalidation; it does NOT own a revise loop.
- Routing pivots on has_children (not ticket type).
- Attestation binding (scope (i)): signature bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit on the tickets branch — edits to description / acceptance-criteria / file_impact / decomposition INVALIDATE (force re-review); tags / comments / links / assignee do NOT (no re-review treadmill). Justification: the cert asserts "process followed against THIS plan"; honoring it after a material edit would falsely certify content never reviewed.
- Certificate semantics (Joe's framing): "we ran our process to the best of our ability given this codebase, and no red flags were raised" — NOT "this plan is perfect." Fail-open is DESIRED. Record COVERAGE (which sound checks ran vs skipped-unsupported) for transparency.
- LAYER 1 — deterministic + structural gate (the ONLY thing that blocks; convergent by construction): frozen binary MET/UNMET rubric. Checks: P1 readiness shape (standardized `## Acceptance Criteria` ALL types), P2 file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/tree-sitter, NOT llm-grep), P3 package/dep existence (manifest/lockfile+registry), P4 oversize signals (config-tunable), P5 task-DAG validity + cross-task interference, P6 AC/DD quality (compound-AND, vague-lexicon, spec-fidelity, verify-command robustness — POLYGLOT FIX: shell-specific verify-cmd sub-checks fail-open/advisory when not POSIX shell), P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalation signal only).
- LAYER 2 — advisory LLM review (NEVER blocks; surfaces findings): the judgment criteria (F1 measurability & agent-in-session-completability, F4 user/problem present, E1 coherence + terminology-consistency + duplicate-requirements, E2 ambiguity + spike/research-undercooked + explicit-decision-deferral, E3 intent fidelity [2-step blind-restate→compare, child-vs-slice], E5 testing-plan completeness, E6 verification/termination + end-state-reachability, G1+G2 edit-set/scope accuracy [target-first then deterministic consumer lookup; high-blast≠fail], G3 child coverage [4-bucket+3-condition], G4 child consistency [7 categories], E4 assumption/premise verification [forced codebase probe, ban cached-context], A1 anti-slop/NIH/config-proliferation/Rule-of-Three) PLUS one BOUNDED broad open-ended pass for unknown-unknowns. Triggered overlays (router-LLM selects relevance): T1 prior-art/novel-arch, T2 empirical probe, T3 integration feasibility, T4 compat/destructiveness-as-explicit-choice, T5a-e perf/reliability/security/a11y/maintainability, T6 UX, T7 docs (+invalidated+hot-path bloat), T8 LLM-prompt antipatterns, T9 shared-state lifecycle (CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE).

KEY REBALANCE (load-bearing, from Opus critical review + Joe): plan review's dominant failure is FALSE-NEGATIVE / rubber-stamp (Kambhampati: LLM judges accept 84% of invalid plans), NOT code-review's false-positive. The elaborate anti-FP contract I'd built was a CODE-REVIEW import (rebar's code-reviewer agents, DSO reviewer-base) and was wrong for plan review. So: RECALL-FIRST — sound/deterministic checks BLOCK; LLM judgment ADVISES/SURFACES; anti-FP stripped to minimal (single benign-reading filter + anti-fabrication instruction). Dropped: cite-or-omit-as-universal-gate (fails for omissions — ground the REQUIREMENT instead, never ask open-ended "what's missing": AbsenceBench) and reachable-harm bar (no impl at plan time — use blockingness-to-criteria severity). Dropped severity-minor-floor (code-review concept, defer to tuning). Dropped per-dimension calibrated abstention (needs a gold set we likely can't get → surface-on-uncertainty instead).

VERDICT MODEL: per-criterion PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL. AMBIGUOUS = non-blocking → escalate to higher-tier adjudicator. FAIL→block per-dimension: sound checks block immediately (near-zero FP); judgment dims advisory (the field keeps LLM plan-review advisory, not blocking, BECAUSE of the rubber-stamp problem). Binary checklists WHERE criteria decompose cleanly (most DET + structural); graded/narrative where genuinely qualitative (intent fidelity, decomposition, the broad pass).

CONFIGURABLE CHUNKING (Joe's reconciliation of cost-vs-quality + local-vs-frontier clients): the partition of criteria across LLM calls is a PROJECT KNOB — fewer/larger calls (frontier models: cost-effective, comparable quality) ↔ more/smaller per-criterion calls (local/small models: less context-limit risk, more reliable). Default "in the middle" (grouped chunks). Replaces the "single structured pass" crystallization. The criteria registry maps to chunks per config.

LAYER-2 LOOP RESOLUTION (Joe caught a muddle): there is NO internal refine loop and NO second broad pass within a single review — the reviewer runs once per invocation and returns findings. The only "loop" is the CLIENT re-invoking after revising. Convergence is FREE because Layer 1 (deterministic, frozen binary rubric) is the SOLE blocker — all-MET is a guaranteed fixed point regardless of advisory findings. On re-invocation, review the CURRENT plan FRESH (don't limit to prior findings when the plan changed). diff-only re-review = OPTIONAL cost-saver, NOT a convergence mechanism. Caveat: the reviewer must NOT be the same model/instance that authored the plan (self-preference bias; generator≠critic).

PROPORTIONATE SCRUTINY + ESCALATION: review depth scales with complexity (small task = DET + one cheap pass; epic/has_children = full depth). Escalation-to-stronger-model factors = SIZE + COMPLEXITY + CENTRALITY (DSO has hooks/lib/centrality.sh + blast-radius-score.py). Risk posture is PROJECT-CONFIGURABLE with a sensible default (to be anchored to DSO ticket data). Budget caps: criterion/chunk-ATOMIC checkpointing (no half-criterion waste; order by priority so a cap sheds lowest-value criteria); Layer 1 always completes (cheap); on Layer-2 budget hit → a NON-TERMINAL "incomplete/inconclusive" outcome — NOT failed (cap is external to the ticket), NOT certified (process didn't complete) — client raises budget / re-runs / escalates.

TOOLING (polyglot, fail-open IRONCLAD): solidlsp (Serena's LSP lib — ALREADY in this repo) + ast-grep (P2 resolution, G1 callers); syft + per-ecosystem registry HTTP + LLM-fallback (P3 deps); scc + lizard (P4 size/CCN); ast-grep/tree-sitter (G2 locus). Unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout → skipped = PASS, NEVER fail; hard "missing" only from top-tier servers (pyright/gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver/clangd). This is the only reason the deterministic tier generalizes across clients. Avoid stack-graphs (archived), Glean (over-engineered); osv-scanner ≠ existence oracle.

EXTENSIBILITY: a dimension is a declarative REGISTRY DESCRIPTOR; the orchestrator is generic (never names a dimension). Reuse rebar's existing rebar.llm extension points (reviewer catalog.json + contracts.py + prompt files + pluggable runner). Criteria registry = frozen WITHIN a review loop (convergence), extended BETWEEN versions (growth). Implement ALL criteria in ONE WAVE (no v1/deferred), decomposed into appropriately-sized tasks with dependencies; dogfood by tracking the decomposition in rebar.

BUGS: PARKED — ship with bug-type tickets SKIPPED + a follow-on epic (their format/content differs markedly from planning tickets; bugs often lack a plan at claim because you investigate first). Revisit after the tuning question. Interim principle: has_plan-driven — a planless ticket is N/A, not FAIL.

OUT OF SCOPE (confirmed): cross-ticket no-overlap / dependency-aware (deterministic file-impact/link now; semantic backlog-scan → follow-on); propose-multiple-then-select loop (authoring, not review); cross-service deploy topology (not in-session-verifiable); DSO's dogfooding/staged-rollout/7-day-monitor validation (violates agent-completable-in-session); all-equal-5 / scalar-trait gating; project-supplied dimensions (FUTURE seam, design-for not built); E8 test-gaming (post-impl, → code-review/close gate).

RESEARCH DONE (~16 subagents): full from-scratch DSO re-exploration (8 chunked agents → lossless dimension catalog; coverage-ledger methodology after an initial single-agent pass missed surfaces); two GitHub/OSS prior-art passes (general LLM-plan-review; LLM-implements-the-plan); LLM-planning failure-modes; scoping + plan-appropriate anti-FP; scoped-vs-broad + convergence; Opus critical review. Key external anchors: AbsenceBench 2506.11440 (LLMs bad at detecting omissions), Kambhampati 2310.08118 (84% false-accept), MAST 2503.13657 (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects), Agent GPA 2510.08847 (coherence as a dimension), CheckEval/TICKing 2410.03608 (binary checklists raise agreement), convergence (cap + severity-gating + diff-only; field keeps LLM plan-review ADVISORY not blocking due to rubber-stamp + non-determinism). Field reality: most ship a single HOLISTIC pass (cost) + HUMAN approval gate (rubber-stamp problem); per-dimension + blocking-LLM-gate is rare — our per-dimension registry is a deliberate, evidenced bet.

ARTIFACTS (job-local /tmp, ephemeral — substance captured here): dso-dimension-catalog-v2.md (lossless DSO dimension union), our-review-dimensions-v2.md / v3.md (design evolution). v4 design-of-record NOT yet written.

OPEN / NEXT: DSO ticket-data mining agent RUNNING — characterize the DSO ticket corpus (sizes/types/complexity/outcomes), extract a stratified example library, propose concrete complexity→depth thresholds + escalation factors (size/complexity/centrality) + chunking default, and assess the corpus as a gold-ish tuning/eval set for over/under-review risk. AFTER it lands → write the v4 design-of-record (Layer-1/Layer-2 + configurable chunking + budget-incomplete status + escalation), then decompose into the rebar implementation epic (one wave, tasks with deps). PENDING JOE: approve the proportionate-depth posture / thresholds, the budget posture, and confirm the bug-skip + follow-on-epic.
